message:
fix: use MultiRepoStorage interface instead of SQLite type assertion for multi-repo sync (dolt-test-in7m)

ExportToMultiRepo/HydrateFromMultiRepo were guarded by type assertions to
*sqlite.SQLiteStorage, causing multi-repo sync to silently skip on the Dolt
backend. Introduces a MultiRepoStorage interface in the storage package
(following the CompactableStorage/BatchDeleter pattern) and adds no-op stubs
on DoltStore since Dolt uses native push/pull instead of JSONL multi-repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+//go:build cgo
+
+package dolt
+
+import "context"
+
+// ExportToMultiRepo is a no-op for Dolt backend.
+// Dolt uses native version control (push/pull/merge) instead of JSONL-based multi-repo sync.
+// Returns nil, nil to indicate multi-repo mode is not active.
+func (s *DoltStore) ExportToMultiRepo(_ context.Context) (map[string]int, error) {
+	return nil, nil
+}
+
+// HydrateFromMultiRepo is a no-op for Dolt backend.
+// Dolt uses native version control (push/pull/merge) instead of JSONL-based multi-repo sync.
+// Returns nil, nil to indicate multi-repo mode is not active.
+func (s *DoltStore) HydrateFromMultiRepo(_ context.Context) (map[string]int, error) {
+	return nil, nil
+}
